assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and either local Docker tooling or access to Ory Network signup.
No Ory Network project and no local Ory Kratos quickstart stack exist yet.

developer_goal: Reach the earliest meaningful first authenticated identity success on Ory, either by creating an Ory Network project or by completing the local Ory Kratos quickstart registration and verification flow.

first_success.outcome: Either an Ory Network developer project is usable for identity flows, or a local Kratos identity is registered, email-verified through MailSlurper, and shows an active verified session.
first_success.observable_signal: For local Kratos: /welcome after verification and /sessions showing verified: true. For Ory Network: unresolved until the selected Network first-success terminal is fixed.

route_selection.surface: Ory Network console versus Ory Kratos local Docker quickstart
route_selection.rule: The audit boundary starts at account creation and prefers self-service web onboarding when one exists; Ory Network signup and local OSS Kratos are consequential peers that must be ranked before counting.

candidates.name: Ory Network free developer project signup
candidates.reason: Official Network docs invite signup for a free developer project and provide a console registration URL, matching the account-creation boundary and self-service web surface, but the complete first authenticated-app terminal after signup is not fixed here.

candidates.name: Ory Kratos local Docker quickstart
candidates.reason: Documents a complete local registration, MailSlurper verification, and /sessions terminal without a SaaS account, but it is not a web account-creation route and conflicts with preferring an existing self-service Network surface.

candidates.name: Ory Network framework quickstarts such as Next.js or Express
candidates.reason: Peer framework guides that assume or create Network projects; they are not ranked as a single universal first path.

required_path.action: Create the first Ory identity-control-plane account or local bootstrap required by the selected route.

required_fields.label: Route-specific account or bootstrap inputs
required_fields.notes: Network signup fields are not fully documented publicly; local Kratos has no SaaS signup and starts with git clone and docker compose.

required_path.observable_result: Either an Ory Network project exists or the local Kratos stack is healthy.

required_path.action: Complete the selected route's first registration or project identity configuration through first success.

required_fields.label: Route-specific identity and verification inputs
required_fields.notes: Local Kratos documents email, name, and password for registration plus MailSlurper verification; Network first-user steps remain unresolved.

required_path.observable_result: The selected route's first authenticated identity outcome is visible.

prerequisites.description: Local Kratos quickstart requires Git, Docker, Docker Compose, and free ports 4455, 4433, 4434, and 4436.
Ory Network requires access to console registration for a developer project.

unavoidable_waits.description: Local docker compose may take minutes before Kratos public and admin HTTP servers report healthy.

platform_outcomes.description: Local quickstart MailSlurper captures verification email for the registered identity.
Ory Network provides a managed identity stack after developer project signup.

excluded.item: Reading Ory documentation as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Production database backends beyond the quickstart SQLite volume
excluded.reason: The Kratos quickstart defers production database setup to later guides.

sources.title: Ory Documentation
evidence_supported: Ory documentation entry and product family context

sources.title: Introduction to Ory Network
evidence_supported: Sign up for a free developer project

uncertainties.question: Is the selected Ory first-mile route Ory Network account creation or the local Ory Kratos Docker quickstart?
uncertainties.impact: Network matches the account-creation and self-service web boundary; local Kratos has a clearer documented verified-session terminal but no SaaS account. The routes are not interchangeable for counts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision that either prioritizes the Network console path and documents its first authenticated terminal, or explicitly accepts local OSS quickstarts when a managed signup also exists.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields and post-signup steps does console.ory.com/registration require before a developer can run a first identity flow?
uncertainties.impact: Without observed Network signup and first-project steps, the account-creation path cannot be verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated read-only walkthrough of Ory Network registration through the earliest official authenticated-app or hosted-flow success.
